degraded.detail: Full shared/tests/ run: 2822 passed, 24 skipped, 1 FAILED (test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact.py::test_arch_impact_drops_found_at_all). Pre-existing + environmental, NOT introduced by this diff: the main-repo .shipwright/agent_docs/decision-drops/ dir exists with 1 drop, 0 of which carry a real architecture_impact (drops aggregated/cleared at the v0.24.0 release d5764ab7), so that test's 'dir present but no arch-impact drops' sanity guard fires. The same condition was recorded by the prior iterate (triage-gc-compliance-refreshed). This diff touches only campaign_init.py + a new test module + campaign-mode.md — neither decision-drops nor architecture.md, so it is structurally impossible for this change to affect that test. Robustness gap (test skips when dir absent but not when present-but-empty) noted in F3a reflection.

surface_verification.justification: cli surface: the new test module is the scripted CLI verification, driving the real campaign_init.py main() against fixture projects (incl. a real triage.jsonl seeded by the real producer). Plus an out-of-band boundary probe ran the installed CLI end-to-end (seed via triage_add.py -> campaign_init --from-triage -> inspect both artifacts). No web/UI surface in this repo; the WebUI consumer lives in shipwright-webui (out of scope).

behaviors.behavior: --expands-triage stamps the snake_case anchor INSIDE the campaign.md YAML frontmatter block (the WebUI join key fm.expands_triage)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_frontmatter_carries_snake_case_key asserts 'expands_triage: <trg>' within md.split('---',2)[1]; born-red before impl, PASSED after

behaviors.behavior: An un-anchored campaign carries NO expands_triage key in either artifact (backward-compat / legacy-shaped)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_omitted_when_not_provided PASSED (status.json + campaign.md both free of the key)

behaviors.behavior: A malformed trg-id is rejected (ValueError from init_campaign; CLI exit 1)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_invalid_trg_id_rejected (pytest.raises ValueError) + ::test_cli_invalid_anchor_returns_1 (ret==1) PASSED; boundary probe step 5 showed exit 1 + clear message

behaviors.behavior: Re-init with the same anchor is idempotent — exactly one anchor line, stable value
The anchor survives the status.json load->modify->dump round-trip on the draft->active flip (touches_io_boundary round-trip)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_anchor_survives_start_roundtrip runs campaign_progress.cmd_start then asserts status['expands_triage'] persists PASSED

behaviors.behavior: --from-triage seeds --intent from the item title/detail (via the real triage SSoT reader) and implies the anchor
behaviors.evidence: ::TestFromTriage::test_from_triage_seeds_intent_and_anchor seeds via real triage.append_triage_item, asserts intent text + anchor in both artifacts PASSED

behaviors.behavior: --from-triage on a non-open (dismissed) item warns but proceeds (does not block re-promotion)
behaviors.evidence: ::test_from_triage_dismissed_item_warns_but_proceeds asserts ret==0, 'WARNING'+'dismissed' on stderr, anchor still written PASSED

behaviors.behavior: Conflicting --from-triage and --expands-triage (different ids) is rejected as an ambiguous anchor (exit 1)
At runtime the real CLI resolves shared/scripts via the walk-up _find_shared_scripts (not the test's manual sys.path injection) and reads a real triage.jsonl
behaviors.evidence: Out-of-band boundary probe: triage_add.py seeded trg-d3a2976f, then `uv run campaign_init.py --from-triage trg-d3a2976f` (no PYTHONPATH help) exited 0 and wrote expands_triage to both artifacts
